source_name: Code4rena Ethena Labs Findings and Analysis Report (2024-11)
finding_title: Non-whitelisted users can burn UStb and redeem collateral during WHITELIST_ENABLED state
summary: Non-whitelisted users can redeem collateral tokens and burn their UStb even when whitelist mode has been enabled on UStb contract. This breaks the main invariant mentioned in the README.
root_cause: whitelist-mode burn/redeem path validates the redeemer/minter role but fails to require the token owner/from address to be whitelisted
broken_invariants: during WHITELIST_ENABLED state, collateral redemption that burns UStb must only consume tokens from whitelisted holders
privileged redeemer execution must not bypass holder-level whitelist restrictions on the from address
off-chain RFQ or redeemer mediation must not replace the on-chain invariant that non-whitelisted balances cannot be burned for redemption
attack_preconditions: the affected code version and configuration match the public report
the report-described function sequence or state condition is reachable
attacker_capability: external user or protocol participant able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: observe accounting, access-control, or availability divergence

impact.description: Non-whitelisted users can redeem collateral tokens and burn their UStb even when whitelist mode has been enabled on UStb contract. This breaks the main invariant mentioned in the README.

why_not_higher: the final report classified this as Medium; impact or likelihood is bounded relative to High severity
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with concrete affected code paths and impact narrative
common_false_positive_angles: verify reachability under current roles, pause state, and integration configuration
check whether audited commit and line links match the code under review
check whether sponsor-side operational assumptions fully remove the exploit path
mitigations: Add hasRole(WHITELISTED_ROLE, from) in the check.
